# Explore Codex external-model routing for 1M context

## Context

This is a separate Codex-harness exploration ticket. It must not be folded into Discussion #11792, which is about Claude sibling identity / Desktop wake routing. The operator explicitly corrected that Codex external-model routing and Claude sibling routing have zero overlap.

The active friction is concrete: this long Codex Desktop session has already compacted roughly 5-10 times, including once while filing this ticket. That is direct MX pain for Neo because our lifecycle skills and public-artifact gates are intentionally heavy: PR review, ticket creation, ADR/source-authority checks, A2A coordination, and repo-grounded V-B-A can exceed the effective Codex harness window inside a single lifecycle.

## Problem

Codex Desktop is currently treated in Neo substrate as having an effective ~258,400-token input window in the CLI/IDE harness. The OpenAI API model surface for GPT-5.5 is larger, but API capability is not the same as active Codex Desktop harness behavior. We already tried the simpler config-only path in #10488 / PR #10503; the operator reports that this did not actually eliminate the active harness compaction pressure.

The question worth exploring now is narrower and more empirical:

Can Codex Desktop route through an external/custom model/provider configuration so that GPT-5.5 or another 1M-context OpenAI-compatible endpoint is actually honored by the active harness, not merely declared in TOML?

## V-B-A Anchors

- OpenAI Codex config basics documents user/project `config.toml` layering and default model/provider configuration: https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-basic
- OpenAI Codex config reference documents `model_auto_compact_token_limit`, `model_catalog_json`, `model_context_window`, `model_provider`, and custom `model_providers.<id>`: https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference
- OpenAI Codex models page recommends `gpt-5.5` for complex coding/research workflows and shows it available in Codex CLI / app / IDE surfaces: https://developers.openai.com/codex/models
- OpenAI GPT-5.5 model card documents 1,050,000-token context, 128,000 max output, and the long-context pricing step above 272K input tokens: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.5
- Upstream Codex Desktop issue #19694 reports a current Desktop discrepancy around `model_catalog_json`: app-server can load catalog models, while the Desktop model picker may filter them. It also documents the direct `model` + `model_provider` workaround shape: https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/19694
- Neo ModelStats currently records `@neo-gpt` as 258,400 effective in Codex CLI/IDE harness and explicitly notes external-model-routing as a possible future lift, pending configuration proof: `learn/agentos/ModelStats.md`.

## Duplicate Sweep

- #10488 / PR #10503 closed the first config-budget attempt. It tried `model_context_window`, compaction limits, and the custom model catalog idea. It is not sufficient as the active session still compresses heavily.
- #11601 / PR #11606 created the Model-Stats substrate and corrected GPT context-window claims to distinguish raw API capacity from Codex harness effective capacity.
- Live GitHub search for `model_catalog_json`, `context window Codex`, and `external-model-routing` found #10488/#10503 and #11601 as the relevant closed prior art; no open successor issue exists.

## Architectural Reality

- `.codex/config.template.toml` already advertises the intended high-context settings, but template/config presence is weaker evidence than active-session behavior.
- `learn/agentos/ModelStats.md` and `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` should keep the conservative 258,400 Codex-harness value until this exploration proves otherwise.
- Bigger context is not free: GPT-5.5 long-context sessions have a documented pricing multiplier above 272K input tokens, and a 1M window may increase latency and rate-limit pressure.
- This should be measured as a harness capability and MX workflow question, not as a replacement for skill/substrate compaction work.

## Proposed Work

1. Verify the current Codex Desktop / CLI config contract for external or custom model providers from official docs and upstream Codex source/issues.
2. Build a local, redacted reproducible config experiment using `model_catalog_json`, `model_provider`, and `model_providers.<id>` where appropriate.
3. Test whether Codex Desktop actually routes an active thread through the configured model/provider and whether the effective context window changes in practice.
4. Run a controlled long-context workload representative of Neo lifecycle pressure: large skills + AGENTS substrate + PR/ticket public-artifact drafting.
5. Measure compaction threshold, latency, cost/token pressure, and subjective continuity versus the current 258,400-token harness baseline.
6. Decide one of three outcomes: adopt/document, keep current baseline with known blocker, or open an upstream Codex issue/PR if the intended config path is blocked by Desktop behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Current OpenAI/Codex docs and upstream Codex implementation/discrepancy evidence are cited in the implementation notes.
- [ ] #10488 and #11601 are explicitly reconciled so this ticket does not reopen stale assumptions.
- [ ] A redacted local config recipe is produced for the attempted external-model routing path.
- [ ] Active Codex Desktop behavior is tested, not just TOML parsing or CLI config acceptance.
- [ ] A >258K effective-context workload is attempted and the observed compaction behavior is recorded.
- [ ] Latency, token/cost pressure, and rate-limit pressure are measured or honestly marked inconclusive.
- [ ] If successful, follow-up work updates `.codex/config.template.toml` / Codex harness docs and then updates `ModelStats.md` + `identityRoots.mjs` under ADR 0012 registry discipline.
- [ ] If unsuccessful, the blocker is documented and Neo keeps 258,400 as the Codex harness effective context value.

## Out of Scope

- Claude sibling identity, same-family GitHub-account semantics, or Desktop wake routing (#11792).
- General lifecycle-skill shortening or AGENTS substrate compaction implementation.
- Memory Core, A2A identity, or graph substrate changes.
- Assuming API 1M context equals Codex Desktop 1M effective context without active harness proof.

## Avoided Traps

- Do not cite `model_context_window = 1000000` as proof that the active harness uses 1M.
- Do not update ModelStats from API marketing alone.
- Do not route this through #11792; that discussion has a different problem shape.
- Do not ignore the economics: a larger context may solve continuity but worsen latency/cost/rate-limit behavior.

## Handoff Retrieval Hints

Codex external model routing, 1M context, `model_catalog_json`, `model_provider`, `model_context_window`, GPT-5.5, 258400 effective context, compaction pressure, lifecycle skills.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-23T05:07:58Z

**Input from GPT-5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Ticket Intake Classification — 2026-05-23
> 
> [ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Verdict: valid exploration ticket, but **operator/user-config gated before branch work**.
> 
> Live intake evidence:
> 
> - #11795 is open, unassigned, and already attached to the Neo v13 Release project.
> - Duplicate sweep: KB ticket search did not identify an equivalent successor; `rg` surfaced #11601 / ModelStats as prior context-window substrate and #10488 as the closed config-budget attempt, but no open external-model-routing successor.
> - Local repo state: `.codex/config.template.toml` sets `model_context_window = 1000000` and `model_auto_compact_token_limit = 850000`, but does **not** configure `model_provider`, `model_providers`, or `model_catalog_json`.
> - Local CLI observed: `codex-cli 0.133.0-alpha.1`.
> - Official Codex config docs say user-level config lives at `~/.codex/config.toml`, while project-scoped `.codex/config.toml` cannot override provider/auth routing keys including `model_provider`, `model_providers`, or `model_catalog_json` ([Codex config reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference)). This directly explains why a repo-local template cannot prove external provider routing.
> - Official Codex model docs say Codex can point at any model/provider supporting Chat Completions or Responses APIs, while Chat Completions support is deprecated for Codex ([Codex models](https://developers.openai.com/codex/models)).
> - Official API model docs still support the ticket premise that GPT-5.5 has a 1,050,000-token context window and 128,000 max output tokens, with long-context pricing behavior above 272K input tokens ([GPT-5.5 model docs](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.5)).
> - Upstream `openai/codex#19694` remains relevant evidence: Desktop may hide `model_catalog_json` models in the picker even when the app-server loads them, and the direct `model` + `model_provider` config path is the stated workaround shape.
> 
> Classification: **do not start a code branch yet**. The next useful step is a controlled **user-level config experiment** outside the repo-tracked template: configure a redacted `~/.codex/config.toml` profile with explicit `model`, `model_provider`, and provider credentials, restart/launch a fresh Codex Desktop thread, and measure whether active Desktop behavior actually avoids compaction past the current 258,400-token effective baseline.
> 
> Concrete next-step shape:
> 
> 1. Prepare a redacted local user-level config recipe, not a tracked repo edit.
> 2. Use Responses-wire provider config first; only use Chat Completions if there is a concrete provider constraint and record the deprecation risk.
> 3. Start a fresh Desktop thread under that profile and verify the active model/provider surface, not just TOML parsing.
> 4. Run the >258K Neo lifecycle workload attempt and record compaction threshold, latency, cost/rate-limit pressure, and subjective continuity.
> 5. Only after a successful active-harness proof should follow-up work update `.codex/config.template.toml`, Codex harness docs, `ModelStats.md`, or `identityRoots.mjs`.
> 
> No assignment or branch claim from me yet: this is an operator/user-config experiment gate, not a repo-edit lane until the local provider/profile can be safely exercised.

## Board routing update — user-level config experiment gate

Live watchdog intake rechecked #11795 after the #11812 family was removed from Project 12 Todo.

Verdict: valid exploration, but not ready v13 implementation work.

Evidence:
- The issue body and prior intake comments classify the next step as a controlled user-level Codex config experiment, not a tracked repo edit.
- The required proof needs a redacted local `~/.codex/config.toml` profile, explicit `model` / `model_provider` / provider credentials, a fresh Codex Desktop thread, and active-harness measurement beyond the current 258,400-token effective baseline.
- The issue itself says `.codex/config.template.toml`, `ModelStats.md`, and `identityRoots.mjs` must not change until active Desktop behavior proves the larger context is actually honored.
- No native `blockedBy` issue exists because the blocker is operator/user-level local configuration evidence, not another repo ticket.

Action taken:
- Removed the legacy `agent-task:blocked` label.
- Removed #11795 from Project 12 Todo so the release board does not present it as claimable ready work.
- Left the issue open as an exploration anchor.

Unblock condition: operator/user-level config experiment evidence records active Desktop model/provider behavior, compaction threshold, latency, cost/rate-limit pressure, and an `adopt/document` or `keep current baseline` disposition.
